input: Design an API strategy for our new SaaS platform that serves mobile and web clients
expected_behavior: Skill activates and produces API style selection (REST vs GraphQL), contract-first workflow with OpenAPI, versioning strategy, security design with OAuth2, and DX plan

input: We have 5 teams contributing to a shared GraphQL API. Help us set up federation.
expected_behavior: Skill activates and recommends GraphQL federation with supergraph architecture, gateway selection (Apollo Router/DGS), subgraph ownership model, and entity references via @key directive

input: We have 50 undocumented REST endpoints with no specs. How do we modernize?
expected_behavior: Skill activates, recommends code-first spec generation from existing endpoints, then contract-first migration with facade pattern, versioning, and deprecation timeline

input: Implement rate limiting for our public API with different tier levels
expected_behavior: Skill activates and produces rate limiting algorithm comparison (token bucket vs sliding window), tier definitions (free/standard/enterprise), 429 response handling, and latency budget (<5ms)

input: Refactor the internal module structure of our Python application
expected_behavior: Skill should NOT activate. This is internal code architecture, not API design. Should route to software-architecture skill instead.
